finding_results.notes: Revised all three confrontation spans (Damian-Voss gallery, Damian floor speech, Loid-Falk comms room). Damian-Voss: replaced polished opener with rougher accusation showing voice arriving flat; shortened Voss's 'vertical thinking' speech to blunt military dismissal; broke supply-chain manifesto with Damian's interrupted processing ('That's—'); added messy pressure beat where Voss interrupts Damian mid-sentence and Damian crumples documents against the railing; replaced quotable exit ('Somebody has to say it first') with involuntary honest 'I know' that unsettles Voss. Damian floor speech: replaced 'billing code for private violence' aphorism with plainer accusatory language; removed narrator-admiring-dialogue ('That line he heard. That line would follow him.') and narrator commentary ('The wording was not graceful. Good.'). Loid-Falk: differentiated Falk from Voss by giving him analyst-briefing reflexes (categorizing names, glasses-cleaning gesture, compartment stammering) instead of philosophical spokesman lines; replaced 'WISE mistake management for morality' and 'if you want arithmetic' with plain 'Twenty years' delivered through mundane physical gesture; replaced 'Men who understand the difference between peace and surrender' and 'I gave you a worldview' with stammered operational excuse and Loid's leverage-shifting Crane callback ('Was he one function too'); replaced 'People tell the truth when glass breaks' with plainer conviction line; replaced 'She kills cleaner than your people write' with stunned 'She's something'; replaced 'I'm an analyst, not a priest' with messy desperate questions about institutional reporting; roughened final diagnosis line. No speaker retains more than one conspicuously aphoristic line; each confrontation now contains at least one messy pressure beat that shifts leverage mid-exchange.

Voss now enters through live-room positioning, exit access, and attention management in the gallery, avoiding a repeat of his earlier build/bearing/grief dossier beat.

Anya's Falk recognition is now tactical and no longer repeats the wallpaper/family-ties setup. The chapter keeps the stronger Loid-side ordinariness/family-betrayal emphasis as the single surviving restatement.

pass_summaries.summary: Expanded and restructured the summit chapter into a clock-coherent multi-front action sequence: Anya now infers the assassination and relay from partial, noisy reads; Damian and Loid's core confrontations stay inside close third; the relay activates only after Loid kills the main board at 14:06; and the final Loid/Anya beat leaves the familial and operational rupture live on the page.

Broke thesis-speech and quotable aphorisms across all three confrontation spans. Voss now sounds like a blunt military operator dismissing a young diplomat rather than a philosophical spokesman. Falk now sounds like a bitter desk analyst whose composure cracks under Crane's name rather than a second version of the same thematic voice. Damian shows visible physical strain (crumpled papers, voice arriving flat, involuntary honesty) rather than performing composed defiance. Added messy leverage-shifting pressure beats: Voss interrupting Damian mid-sentence, Falk stammering on compartment language, Loid weaponizing Falk's own 'one function' phrasing against him via Crane. Removed narrator admiration of dialogue lines. Reduced each speaker to at most one strong statement, with the rest arriving rough, reactive, or procedural.

Completed the chapter_15 prose/copyedit pass by addressing all packet findings: reduced the mechanical clipped-fragment tic in the flagged action/consequence beats, reframed Voss's entrance around the immediate confrontation, and removed the duplicate Anya-side Falk ordinariness/family-ties beat while preserving the stronger Loid-side payoff.
